title: Setting DNS of a container does not work in docker-compose
body: ### Issue Description I am trying to make the example about [accessing localstack via endpoint url](https://docs.localstack.cloud/aws/capabilities/networking/accessing-endpoint-url/#from-your-container) which needs to set one of the containers in the compose services as the default dns. I used rootfull, rootless containers also used docker-compose and podman-compose but non of them worked so far. I can see the DNS setting when I inspect the pod, but DNS queries still goes to 10.0.2.1 podman version 5.6.2 Docker Compose version 2.40.3 limactl version 2.0.1 macOS - 15.7.1 ### Steps to reproduce the issue Steps to reproduce the issue 1. Use the docker-compose file [here](https://docs.localstack.cloud/aws/capabilities/networking/accessing-endpoint-url/#from-your-container) 2. Use docker-compose or podman-compose 3. Try to resolve localhost.localstack.cloud from the second container ### Describe the results you received Container is still using 10.0.2.1 for DNS resolutions ### Describe the results you expected Container is still using 10.0.2.20 for DNS resolutions ### podman info output ```yaml Client: APIVersion: 5.6.0 BuildOrigin: pkginstaller Built: 1755272543 BuiltTime: Fri Aug 15 17:42:23 2025 GitCommit: da671ef6cfa3fc9ac6225c18f1dd0a70a951e43f GoVersion: go1.25.0 Os: darwin OsArch: darwin/arm64 Version: 5.6.0 host: arch: arm64 buildahVersion: 1.41.5 cgroupControllers: - cpu - io - memory - pids cgroupManager: systemd cgroupVersion: v2 conmon: package: conmon-2.1.13-1.fc42.aarch64 path: /usr/bin/conmon version: 'conmon version 2.1.13, commit: ' cpuUtilization: idlePercent: 98.5 systemPercent: 0.54 userPercent: 0.96 cpus: 4 databaseBackend: sqlite distribution: distribution: fedora variant: coreos version: "42" emulatedArchitectures: - container_id: 0 host_id: 501 size: 1 - container_id: 1 host_id: 100000 size: 1000000 kernel: 6.15.10-200.fc42.aarch64 linkmode: dynamic logDriver: journald memFree: 1350823936 memTotal: 3788349440 networkBackend: netavark networkBackendInfo: backend: netavark dns: package: aardvark-dns-1.16.0-1.fc42.aarch64 path: /usr/libexec/podman/aardvark-dns version: aardvark-dns 1.16.0 package: netavark-1.16.1-1.fc42.aarch64 path: /usr/libexec/podman/netavark version: netavark 1.16.1 ociRuntime: name: crun package: crun-1.23.1-1.fc42.aarch64 path: /usr/bin/crun version: |- crun version 1.23.1 commit: d20b23dba05e822b93b82f2f34fd5dada433e0c2 rundir: /run/user/501/crun spec: 1.0.0 +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +LIBKRUN +WASM:wasmedge +YAJL os: linux pasta: executable: /usr/sbin/pasta package: passt-0^20250805.g309eefd-2.fc42.aarch64 version: | pasta 0^20250805.g309eefd-2.fc42.aarch64-pasta Copyright Red Hat GNU General Public License, version 2 or later This is free software: you are free to change and redistribute it. comments.body: Last I checked, compose places all containers in custom networks, which should all have aardvark-dns enabled. When Aardvark is in use, containers will have it set as their primary DNS server (to ensure that we can reach other containers in the network by name) but, for queries not on the local network, aardvark will forward to the container's specified DNS servers. Given this, I'm inclined to say this is working as designed. Is there anything that 10.0.2.20 would know that other DNS servers would not that you can use to check that forwarding is working as advertised?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
